title: The Shape of the Land: DEM to TWI
description: Compute the Topographic Wetness Index from a DEM step by step — click cells to trace D8 flow paths, explore the result on a 3-D draggable terrain, and compare elevation vs wetness surfaces side by side with a wipe slider. Includes D8 vs D∞ comparison and a detailed explainer on why ln() matters.

citation.text: Beven, K.J. & Kirkby, M.J. (1979). A physically based, variable contributing area model of basin hydrology. Hydrol. Sci. Bull., 24(1), 43–69.

author: Mohammad Galib, Purdue University
